text.title: GA4 Agent Gotchas and Anti-patterns
text.summary: Common failures when agents instrument or query GA4: param limits, dedup, quota exhaustion, consent mode, and cross-domain.

antipattern.description: purchase event fires without transaction_id parameter.
antipattern.reason: Without transaction_id, GA4 cannot deduplicate purchase events. If the page reloads or the event fires twice, revenue is double-counted. Always include transaction_id in every purchase event.

antipattern.description: Agent generates event/parameter names longer than 40 characters from product specs.
antipattern.reason: GA4 silently truncates names. Build a validator into the taxonomy codegen step: assert len(name) <= 40 for every event and parameter name before deploying.

antipattern.description: DebugView used to confirm historical events are tracking correctly.
antipattern.reason: DebugView only shows real-time events. Standard reports have a 24-48h lag. An agent asking "why isn't my event in reports?" must wait, not re-debug the implementation.

antipattern.description: Service account JSON keys passed as a parameter in agent prompts.
antipattern.reason: JSON keys in prompts leak credentials into LLM context logs. Use Workload Identity Federation or short-lived OAuth tokens; never hard-code key material.

antipattern.description: Comparing pre-Consent-Mode vs post-Consent-Mode periods as a performance regression.
antipattern.reason: Consent Mode v2 changes reported data dramatically (EU traffic can drop 30-50%). An agent must check consent mode rollout dates before attributing declines to campaign performance.

antipattern.description: Agent-generated single-domain gtag code deployed on a multi-domain site.
antipattern.reason: Without the linker config (gtag 'linker' parameter), users crossing domains appear as new sessions with lost attribution. Always check if the site spans multiple domains before generating gtag code.

rule.statement: "Conversions" in GA4 reports only counts events that were explicitly marked as conversions in Admin; an agent reporting "conversions" from a property where no events are marked returns zero.
rule.rationale: GA4 does not automatically infer conversion intent from event names. An agent must verify which events are marked as conversions in Admin before interpreting conversion metrics.
